pages.title: Overview
pages.body:
A neon ARTLAB logotype pulses over a dark grid backdrop, with cycling inspirational quotes beneath it. All text is rendered each frame onto an off-screen HTML canvas, which is uploaded as a Three.js CanvasTexture onto a transparent full-screen quad in front of the scene geometry.

Navigate through the pages below to walk through each section of this file.

pages.title: Setup — Canvas Texture & Quad Mesh
pages.body:
setup() builds the scene geometry first: a dark background plane and two sets of thin emissive strips (13 horizontal, 21 vertical) that form a barely-visible grid recessed behind the main content. A 1280×720 canvas element is created and wrapped in a CanvasTexture; the resulting texture drives a transparent MeshBasicMaterial on a full-screen quad placed slightly in front of the grid.

Using a canvas texture rather than Text geometry means any 2D drawing API call — blurs, gradients, arcs — is available for free without loading a font loader.

pages.title: Update — Glow Passes
pages.body:
Each frame the canvas is cleared with a translucent black fill (opacity 1.0, so it is a full clear) and the logotype is redrawn in four stacked passes: two heavily-blurred wide halos, a tighter medium halo, the sharp fill layer, and a thin stroke. Stacking blurred copies at decreasing radii simulates the bloom effect that would otherwise require a postprocessing render target.

All colours share the same rgba() alpha, which is multiplied by a 0.8 Hz sine pulse so the glow breathes over time.

pages.title: Update — Quote Cycling & Fade
pages.body: Quotes rotate on a QUOTE_INTERVAL timer: when the time since the last swap exceeds 4 seconds the index advances and the clock resets. A local fade coefficient is derived from the time within the current interval — linearly ramping up during the first 0.6 s and ramping down during the last 0.6 s — so each quote fades in and out smoothly. The quote is drawn twice: once with a 1.5 px blur for a soft glow, then again sharp on top, with the fade applied to both alpha values.

pages.title: Teardown
pages.body: teardown() disposes the CanvasTexture to release the GPU memory it occupies. The canvas element itself is garbage-collected when ctx._canvas and ctx._canvasCtx go out of scope.
